content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of length NNN.

You have to partition the elements of the array into some subsequences such that:


Each element AiA_iAi​ (1≤i≤N)(1 \le i \le N)(1≤i≤N) belongs to **exactly one** subsequence.
The mean of the mean of subsequences is **maximised**.
Formally, let S1,S2,…,SKS_1, S_2, \dots, S_KS1​,S2​,…,SK​ denote KKK subsequences of array AAA such that each element AiA_iAi​ (1≤i≤N)(1 \le i \le N)(1≤i≤N) belongs to exactly one subsequence SjS_jSj​ (1≤j≤K)(1 \le j \le K)(1≤j≤K).

Let XjX_jXj​ (1≤j≤K)(1 \le j \le K)(1≤j≤K) denote the mean of the elements of subsequence SjS_jSj​. You need to maximise the value ∑j=1KXjK\frac{\sum_{j=1}^K X_j}{K}K∑j=1K​Xj​​.

Print the maximum value. The answer is considered correct if the relative error is less than 10−610^{-6}10−6.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
First line of each test case consists of a single integer NNN - denoting the length of array AAA.
Second line of each test case consists of NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ - denoting the array AAA.


Output Format
For each test case, output in a single line, the **maximum** possible mean of mean of subsequences. The answer is considered correct if the relative error is less than 10−610^{-6}10−6.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 3⋅1053 \cdot 10^53⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 111: We can partition the array in 222 ways - ({10},{20})(\{10\}, \{20\})({10},{20}) or ({10,20})(\{10,20\})({10,20}). In both cases, mean of mean of subsequences comes out to be 151515.
Test Case 222: The optimal partition is ({1,2},{3})(\{1, 2\}, \{3\})({1,2},{3}).

Mean of first subsequence =1+22=1.5 = \frac{1+2}{2} = 1.5=21+2​=1.5.
Mean of second subsequence =31=3 = \frac{3}{1} = 3=13​=3.

Thus, mean of mean of subsequences is 1.5+32=4.52=2.25\frac{1.5+3}{2} = \frac{4.5}{2} = 2.2521.5+3​=24.5​=2.25.
Test Case 333: Any partition will yield the mean of mean of subsequences as 505050.
